2606.30026.title: MuseBench: Benchmarking Intent-Level Audiovisual Arts Understanding in MLLMs
2606.30026.paper_summary: Audiovisual arts encompass diverse creative disciplines, including cinema, visual arts, stage performance, and game design, where artistic meaning arises from deliberate combinations of visual, auditory, and narrative elements (e.g., fear amplified through claustrophobic framing, or grief conveyed through silence and lingering close-ups). True artistic understanding extends beyond recognizing what is depicted to reasoning about why it is expressed through particular creative choices. Despite the strong progress of multimodal large language models (MLLMs), this critical aspect of artistic understanding remains underexplored, as existing benchmarks largely measure perceptual recognition while overlooking reasoning about creative intent. To address this gap, we introduce Musebench, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate MLLMs on nuanced artistic understanding. It comprises 4,016 questions spanning cinematic arts, static visual arts, stage performing arts, and game arts, distilled from over 10K candidate video essays that pair professional commentary with visual demonstration. To capture the open-ended nature of artistic analysis at scale, the benchmark combines single-select and variable-option multi-select questions. All questions are generated and refined through a four-phase iterative pipeline combining shortcut filtering, adversarial distractors, and expert validation. Comprehensive zero-shot evaluation of 28 state-of-the-art MLLMs reveals that even the best-performing model achieves only 48.29% accuracy, substantially below human expert performance of 87.18%, exposing a significant gap in current models' creative domain expertise.
2606.30026.paper_summary_zh: 視聽藝術涵蓋多樣的創意學科，包括電影、視覺藝術、舞台表演和遊戲設計，其中藝術意義源於視覺、聽覺和敘事元素的精心組合（例如，通過幽閉恐懼的構圖放大恐懼，或通過沉默和持續的特寫傳達悲傷）。真正的藝術理解超越了認識所描繪的內容，還包括對為何通過特定創意選擇來表達這些內容的推理。儘管多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）取得了顯著進展，但藝術理解的這一關鍵方面仍然未被充分探索，因為現有基準主要測量感知識別，而忽略了對創意意圖的推理。為了填補這一空白，我們推出了Musebench，一個全面的基準，旨在評估MLLMs在細緻藝術理解方面的能力。它包含4,016個問題，涵蓋電影藝術、靜態視覺藝術、舞台表演藝術和遊戲藝術，這些問題提煉自超過10K的候選視頻論文，這些論文將專業評論與視覺展示相結合。為了在大規模上捕捉藝術分析的開放性質，該基準結合了單選和可變選項的多選問題。所有問題都是通過一個四階段的迭代流程生成和精煉的，該流程結合了快捷過濾、對抗性干擾項和專家驗證。對28個最先進的MLLMs進行的全面零樣本評估顯示，即使是表現最好的模型也僅達到48.29%的準確率，遠低於人類專家的87.18%表現，揭示了當前模型在創意領域專業知識上的重大差距。
2606.30026.author: Yuxuan Fan et.al.
2606.30026.authors: Yuxuan Fan, Gyusik Seo, Jing Hao, Jaemin Cho, Mohit Bansal, Jaehong Yoon
